summary: Iteration 41 - Google Places Integration Testing. Backend: 8/8 tests PASS (100%). Frontend: All UI tests PASS. Admin panel 'Crear Residencias' tab has Place ID field with 'Buscar' button. POST /api/admin/residencias/create correctly accepts and stores latitude, longitude, google_rating, google_total_reviews, google_reviews fields. Provider profile page shows 'Reseñas de Google' section with reviews and 'Ubicación' section with map (Google Maps shows error due to billing restrictions - expected).

issues: Google Maps shows 'Oops! Something went wrong' error due to API key billing restrictions. This is expected per test request. Fallback link 'Ver en Google Maps' only shows when API fails to load, not when it loads but fails to render.

backend_api_tests: GET /api/providers/{id} returns provider with google_reviews, google_rating, google_total_reviews, latitude, longitude fields
Provider cc9f5f3a-a2ea-4108-b358-d12e9448e746 has coordinates (-37.47, -72.37) and 5 Google reviews
POST /api/admin/residencias/create accepts Google Place data (lat, lng, rating, reviews)
Created residencia correctly stores google_rating=4.5, google_total_reviews=25, 2 reviews
GET /api/providers/{new_id} verifies Google data persisted correctly
POST /api/admin/residencias/create works without Google data (defaults to 0)
GET /api/admin/google-place/{place_id} endpoint exists (returns 400 for invalid place_id)
frontend_ui_tests: 'Buscar' button present next to Place ID field (data-testid='fetch-google-place-btn')
Provider profile page loads for provider with Google data
5 Google reviews displayed with star ratings
Map container present (Google Maps error expected due to billing)

critical_code_review_comments: Backend ResidenciaCreate model correctly includes all Google fields: latitude, longitude, google_rating, google_total_reviews, google_reviews
Backend create_residencia endpoint stores Google data in provider document and returns google_data in response
Frontend AdminPanel.jsx correctly fetches Google Place data via PlacesService.getDetails and sends to backend
Frontend ProviderProfile.jsx correctly displays google_reviews section when provider has google_reviews array
Frontend SafeMap component shows fallback only when API fails to load, not when it loads but fails to render (acceptable limitation)
All data-testid attributes properly implemented for testing
seed_data_creation: Created TEST_ prefixed residencias for testing (TEST_Google_Residencia_*, TEST_NoGoogle_Residencia_*, TEST_FullGoogle_*)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 41: Google Places Integration COMPLETE. Backend stores google_rating, google_total_reviews, google_reviews, latitude, longitude in providers collection. Frontend AdminPanel has Place ID field with Buscar button that fetches Google Places data. ProviderProfile shows 'Reseñas de Google' section and 'Ubicación' map section. Google Maps API key has billing restrictions so map shows error (expected). Test provider: cc9f5f3a-a2ea-4108-b358-d12e9448e746 (Fundación De Beneficencia Hogar De Cristo) has 5 Google reviews and coordinates.

mocked_apis.notes: Google Maps API key has billing restrictions - map rendering fails with RefererNotAllowedMapError (expected per test request)
